papers.title: Epigenetic Clocks of Biological Aging and Risk of Incident Mild Cognitive Impairment and Dementia: The Women's Health Initiative Memory Study
papers.summary: Horvath and colleagues used multiple epigenetic clocks to predict dementia risk in the WHI cohort. Accelerated epigenetic aging associated with increased MCI and dementia risk.

papers.title: Supramolecular delivery of senolytics enables targeted anti-senescence therapy and accelerated fracture healing
papers.summary: New supramolecular delivery system for targeted senolytic therapy. Selective clearance of senescent cells at fracture sites, accelerating bone healing with reduced off-target effects.

papers.title: Mitochondrial bioenergetics-SASP crosstalk determines senolytic efficacy in therapy-induced senescence
papers.summary: Mitochondrial bioenergetics and SASP signalling interact to determine senolytic efficacy. Metabolic profiling could predict and improve senolytic drug selection.

papers.title: Senescent endothelial cells: key commanders of cellular communication within atherosclerotic plaques
papers.summary: Senescent endothelial cells orchestrate intercellular communication in atherosclerotic plaques, driving plaque instability. Positions endothelial senescence as cardiovascular aging target.

papers.title: Human cytomegalovirus infection and cognitive decline
papers.summary: CMV linked to accelerated cognitive decline. CMV DNA in 93% of vascular dementia brains vs 34% of controls. Clearing CMV may be a viable anti-aging immune strategy.

papers.title: Levetiracetam reduces amyloid-beta production via synaptic vesicle pathway
papers.summary: FDA-approved epilepsy drug reduces Aβ42 production by modifying SV cycling. Restored abnormalities and reduced synapse loss in mice. Potential for early Alzheimer's prevention with existing drug.

papers.title: Negative social ties as risk factors for accelerated aging, inflammation, and multimorbidity
papers.summary: PNAS study: negative social relationships associated with accelerated biological aging and higher multimorbidity. Social environment as modifiable aging factor.

papers.title: Epigenetic and microbiome responses to greens supplementation in obese older adults
papers.summary: RCT showing greens supplementation altered DNA methylation and gut microbiome in obese older adults. Dietary interventions can modify epigenetic aging markers.

papers.title: Aggregating Epigenetic Clocks to Study Human Capital Formation
papers.summary: Novel approach aggregating multiple epigenetic clocks to study how socioeconomic factors affect biological aging via DunedinPACE.

papers.title: Economic impact of biomarker-based aging interventions on healthcare costs
papers.summary: Models economic impact of controlling aging pace through biomarker monitoring. Cost-effectiveness framework for longevity interventions using DunedinPACE.
